steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage claims energy costs prevent full-speed use even during escape — the highest-stakes scenario. The graph data must show animals running below maximum speed when fleeing a predator.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: shows similar speed percentages across contexts but doesn't isolate the energy-constraint explanation for escape specifically. ✗
  • Choice B — multiple species use 30–90% of maximal speed when escaping: directly shows animals don't reach full speed even under predation pressure, which is exactly the evidence that energy costs constrain escape speed. ✓
  • Choice C: suggests high-speed use predominates, contradicting the passage's claim. ✗
  • Choice D: focuses on pursuit behavior rather than escape, missing the passage's specific claim. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
